summary: DHH posts a straightforward rundown of Apple's new PowerBook announcements from Macworld, covering both the 17-inch and 12-inch Titanium PowerBook models. The post lists hardware specifications including screen resolution, processor speed, graphics cards, and battery life. It's an early blog post that reads as live notes from an Apple keynote, with minimal commentary beyond calling the backlight keyboard 'very cool.' The post reflects DHH's early blogging style as a simple tech enthusiast before his later prominence.
key_points: Apple announced a 17-inch Titanium PowerBook at $3299 with a 1 GHz G4 processor and FireWire 880
A more affordable 12-inch Titanium PowerBook was priced at $1799 with an 867 MHz G4
Both models featured backlit keyboards, which DHH found notably impressive
The post is a pure spec dump with almost no editorial commentary or opinion
money_quotes: Backlight keyboard (very cool, it senses when it's dark and turns it on)
key_insight: An early, minimalist blog post capturing Apple hardware specs with little of the opinionated voice DHH would later become known for.
